# W30T2 Implementation Plan

## Task Goal

Add module-level documentation for every public Xenon module so each docs.rs module page explains responsibilities, key concepts, and relationships to neighboring modules. This task delivers the `//!` overview layer between crate docs and item docs.

## Related Design Documents

- `design/29-documentation.md` §3.1
- `design/29-documentation.md` §5.1-§5.3
- `design/29-documentation.md` §7, Wave 2 T5-T7
- `design/29-documentation.md` §8.2-§8.4
- `design/29-documentation.md` §9.1-§9.2
- `design/01-architecture.md` §3, module tree

## Dependencies

- W30T1

- `src/dimension/mod.rs`
- `src/element/mod.rs`
- `src/complex/mod.rs`
- `src/storage/mod.rs`
- `src/layout/mod.rs`
- `src/tensor/mod.rs`
- `src/iter/mod.rs`
- `src/math/mod.rs`

## Implementation Details

### Module groups and required themes

Follow the grouped Wave 2 task split in `design/29-documentation.md` §7:

- core modules: `dimension`, `element`, `complex`, `storage`, `layout`
- tensor and operation modules: `tensor`, `iter`, `math`, `overload`, `broadcast`, `reduction`, `matrix`, `shape`, `index`, `construct`, `set`
- infrastructure modules: `ffi`, `workspace`, `error`, `prelude`, `convert`, `format`

### Documentation content per module

Each public `mod.rs` page should include:

- module responsibility summary
- core concepts or type relationships
- notable public entry points
- feature-effect notes where behavior changes under `simd` or `parallel`
- links or references to adjacent modules where appropriate

`simd` and `parallel` remain internal backends. Document their public API effects through the outward-facing modules instead of creating separate public-surface module docs.

### Example snippet expectations

At least one representative module-level example should appear where it clarifies the module purpose, for example:

- constructor examples in `src/construct/mod.rs`
- broadcast example in `src/broadcast/mod.rs`
- indexing example in `src/index/mod.rs`
- formatting example in `src/format/mod.rs`
- workspace borrow example in `src/workspace/mod.rs`

## Test Plan

- `cargo doc --no-deps`
- `RUSTDOCFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo doc --all-features --no-deps`
- spot-check generated module pages for missing-doc and broken-link warnings

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Every public module listed above has a `//!` overview
- [ ] Module docs explain responsibilities and core concepts, not just file names
- [ ] Feature notes distinguish always-present APIs from feature-affected behavior
- [ ] Internal `simd` and `parallel` details are documented only through their impact on public APIs
- [ ] `cargo doc` succeeds without missing-module-doc warnings

## Development Workflow

1. Start with core modules, since later module docs build on their terminology.
2. Add tensor and operation module overviews next.
3. Finish with infrastructure modules such as `ffi`, `workspace`, `convert`, `format`, `error`, and `prelude`.
4. Run `cargo doc` and fix missing-doc or broken-link issues before moving to item-level docs.
